tmux: A terminal multiplexer that lets you split terminal sessions into multiple panes, run tasks in the background, and keep sessions active when disconnected.
git: A distributed version control system that tracks file changes and coordinates work on shared code repositories.
docker: A containerization platform that packages software into standardized units (containers) containing everything needed to run it.
kubernetes: An open-source orchestration system for automating containerized application deployment, scaling, and management.
recursion: A programming technique where a function calls itself directly or indirectly to solve a problem by breaking it into smaller sub-problems.
api: Application Programming Interface. A set of rules and protocols that allows different software applications to communicate with each other.
npm: Node Package Manager. The default package manager for the JavaScript runtime environment Node.js, hosting millions of reusable code packages.
ssh: Secure Shell. A cryptographic network protocol for operating network services securely over an unsecured network, typically used for remote CLI logins.
sql: Structured Query Language. A standardized programming language used to manage, query, and manipulate relational databases.
rest api: A web API that adheres to Representational State Transfer constraints, allowing interaction with stateless, resource-oriented endpoints.
cli: Command Line Interface. A text-based user interface used to run programs, manage files, and interact with the computer using commands.
